coverage: 100% of reachable node types; the browser evaluates all 1,007 nodes in the root closure.
validation: Four Blender/VM cases cover authored settings, wrapper switching, thickness, and wrapper dimensions. Blender-float Newell face normals and safe-acos corner weights recover 38 authored vertices and make the authored face count exact: 61,697 / 53,892 versus Blender's 61,812 / 53,892, with bounds within 0.0303 units. Thickness 6 is 41,857 / 33,417 versus 42,031 / 33,434, but one outer Y extent remains 0.9862 units short; the 60 by 45 wrapper is 38,240 / 33,680 versus 38,301 / 33,678 and within 0.0100 units. Corrected multi-output nested probes establish the upstream boundary directly: shrink receives a bit-exact 57,757 / 57,276 mesh, and its first Raycast-driven Set Position introduces the full position residual. Raycast selects exactly the same 52,555 hits as Blender. Its Object Info target is topology-exact at 129,116 / 126,833 but differs from the first vertex onward, with 0.0000453 maximum error and 0.0000239 RMS error; the near-tangent projection amplifies this to a 0.006813 hit-position delta. A freshly extracted dump containing all 57 full-precision matrix_world values does not improve the target (0.0000496 maximum, 0.0000260 RMS), ruling out missing dump matrices as the correction by itself. Merge by Distance and edge proximity remain verified exact on Blender upstream geometry; the unresolved piece is Blender's evaluated relative dependency-transform construction, not weld, proximity, autosmooth, normal tolerances, or epsilon snapping.
